  • Method Details

    • findCustomImports

      public static List<com.github.javaparser.ast.ImportDeclaration> findCustomImports(com.github.javaparser.ast.CompilationUnit existingCompilationUnit, com.github.javaparser.ast.CompilationUnit newCompilationUnit)
      Compare two compilation units and find imports that are in the existing file but not in the new file. We assume this means they are required for some custom method, so we will add them to the new file if there are other items to merge. This may create unused imports in the new file if the initial assumption is incorrect, but better safe than sorry.
      Parameters:
      existingCompilationUnit - compilation unit representing the existing file
      newCompilationUnit - compilation unit representing the new file

    • membersMatch

      public static boolean membersMatch(com.github.javaparser.ast.body.BodyDeclaration<?> member1, com.github.javaparser.ast.body.BodyDeclaration<?> member2)
      Compare two members to see if they are "functionally equivalent". This is defined as:
      • Members are the same type
      • Members have the same signature or basic declaration
      Parameters:
      member1 - the first member
      member2 - the second member
      Returns:
      true if the members are functionally equivalent

    • findCustomSuperInterfaces

      public static List<com.github.javaparser.ast.type.ClassOrInterfaceType> findCustomSuperInterfaces(com.github.javaparser.ast.body.BodyDeclaration<?> existingType, com.github.javaparser.ast.body.BodyDeclaration<?> newType)

    • findSuperInterfaces

      public static List<com.github.javaparser.ast.type.ClassOrInterfaceType> findSuperInterfaces(com.github.javaparser.ast.body.BodyDeclaration<?> bodyDeclaration)

    • addSuperInterface

      public static void addSuperInterface(com.github.javaparser.ast.body.BodyDeclaration<?> bodyDeclaration, com.github.javaparser.ast.type.ClassOrInterfaceType superInterface)

    • stringify

      public static String stringify(com.github.javaparser.ast.ImportDeclaration importDeclaration)
      Create a string representation of an import that we can use to find matches.
      Parameters:
      importDeclaration - the import declaration to stringify
      Returns:
      string representation of the import (not a full import statement)

    • stringify
